rulings.Case Title: TYRONE L. ADAMS VS. CHARLES L. EASLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renewed Motion And Application Requesting Reconsideration Of Plaintiff Motion For Jury Trial Date Against Defendant, Attorney John Judson Waggoman
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 7, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF TYRONE ADAMS Renewed Motion And Application Requesting Reconsideration Of Plaintiff Motion For Jury Trial Date Against Defendant, Attorney John Judson Waggoman is DENIED. Plaintiff's original motion to set a jury trial was denied on January 3, 2025 because the case as to the Waggoman Defendants was dismissed. Plaintiff moves under CCP section 1008, but cites no new facts or circumstances since that dismissal. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SOMALIA FOLASHADE ET AL VS. THE SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT GREYSTAR CALIFORNIA, INC., FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PURPOSES OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
rulings.Rulings: (Part 2 of 2) Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 7, 2025 line 8. DEFENDANT GREYSTAR CALIFORNIA, INC., FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES, LLC DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT The demurrer to the Fifth Cause of Action for Violation of Civil Code section 51 is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for the same reasons as the First cause of action, i.e., there is nothing in the FAC supporting that Mr. Chapman requested an accommodation that he did not receive. The demurrer to the Sixth Cause of Action for Breach of the Warranty of Habitability is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiffs to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action. The demurrer to the Seventh Cause of Action for Constructive Eviction is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiffs to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action. The demurrer to the Eighth Cause of Action for Negligence and Negligence per se is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiffs to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action. The demurrer to the Ninth, Tenth, and Eleventh Causes of Action are OVERRULED to the extent that they were alleged against defendant SFHA, and not defendants Fillmore Center and Greystar. The demurrer to the Twelfth Cause of Action for Fraud and Misrepresentation is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action with specificity; specifically, who made the misrepresentation and when and to whom. The demurrer to the Thirteenth Cause of Action for IIED is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiff to allege facts regarding the contents of the discriminatory remarks and who they were made by and to whom. The demurrer to the Fourteenth Cause of Action for Breach of Contract is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiffs to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action. Specifically, details regarding which purported contract Plaintiffs are relying on and the terms of that contract that require Defendants to add Ms. Folashade as a tenant and provide a lifetime lease to Mr. Chapman. Plaintiffs request to file a late opposition is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SOMALIA FOLASHADE ET AL VS. THE SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: (Part 1 of 2 ) Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 7, 2025 line 8. DEFENDANT GREYSTAR CALIFORNIA, INC., FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES, LLC DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend on grounds of uncertainty and for failure to state a cause of action. Plaintiffs shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint clearly labeling which causes of action are asserted by Mr. Chapman, Ms. Folashade, or both; and which defendant the cause of action is asserted against. In addition, the demurrer to the First Cause of Action for Violation of the Fair Housing Act is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. On the one hand, Plaintiffs allege that Defendants denied Mr. Chapman's request for transfer to an accessible unit but 16 alleges Defendants did offer Plaintiff Chapman an accessible unit but it was rejected. Moreover, Plaintiffs' own facts demonstrate that it is SFHA that must approve any requests for "below market rent" for the new unit, and therefore defendants Fillmore Center and Greystar do not have the requisite authority to grant the relief requested. These allegations must be reconciled. The demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for Violation of the Americans with Disabilities Act is OVERRULED. The court in Independent Housing Services of San Francisco v. Fillmore Center Associates (1993) 840 F.Supp. 1328, cited by Defendants, states that the ADA does apply to the Fillmore Center. (Id. at 1344.) The demurrer to the Third Cause of Action for Violations of the California Fair Employment and Housing Act is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for the same reasons as the First cause of action, i.e., there is nothing in the FAC supporting that Mr. Chapman requested an accommodation that he did not receive. The demurrer to the Fourth Cause of Action for Violations of the California Disabled persons Act is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for the same reasons as the First cause of action, i.e., there is nothing in the FAC supporting that Mr. Chapman requested an accommodation that he did not receive. (See part 2 of 2 - tentative ruling continued)

rulings.Case Title: 905 COLUMBUS AVENUE, LLC VS. JOHN COMPTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant John Compton To Respond To Written Discovery, Produce Documents, And To Deem Matters Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 7, 2025 line 12. PLAINTIFF 905 COLUMBUS AVENUE, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant John Compton To Respond To Written Discovery, Produce Documents, And To Deem Matters Admitted is GRANTED in part, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objection to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories-General (Set One); Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer (Set One); Special Interrogatories (Set One); and Request for Production of Documents (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Truth of all matters in the Request for Admissions (Set One) are deemed admitted. Request to compel production of documents is denied as premature. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
